_meta.note: Schema v2: each system has planets[] (each planet may carry moons[] and other_objects[]) plus system-level other_objects[]. Every object: {name, source: book|catalog, type, status, first, description}; catalog planets also carry method/year/host/mass_earth/radius_earth/sources. System-level: description, binary ({mode: close|far, components:[spectral,...]} or null), confirmed_planets (catalog count), book_mentions. Edit with editor.html.

book_mentions.first: A3 Ch 085 - Planetary Conquest And Repairs
A1 Ch 028 - Fog Of War Part 2

planets.name: Ackman
planets.description:
A frozen planet. The main exports are rare hydrogen isotopes mined from the ice.
Gravity: 0.731 g.
Average temperature: -175 C°
Hydro extent: 99%
Population: ~3000

other_objects.name: Ackman Station
other_objects.description: An orbital facility in an equatorial geosynchronous orbit over the planet's single city. Managed by Portmaster Whitely.

other_objects.name: Ackman Asteroid Belt
other_objects.description: Aa extensive asteroid belt containing valuable metals

planets.name: First planet
planets.description: A small rocky planet close to its star.

planets.name: Second planet
planets.description: A small rocky planet close to its star.

planets.name: Third planet
planets.description: A small rocky planet close to its star.

planets.name: Dedia IV
planets.description: A colony world, with around 60% of the surface covered by liquid water forming massive oceans. It has three major continents broken up by massive mountain ranges in the center of each, with large river networks flowing out from them. The human colony is situated in a small area near the equator on one of the continents. The largest city, Dedia Prime, is connected to orbit with a space elevator. The colony is managed by Governor John Tyler. The planet is also inhabited by a native species of Rexxor, an large insectoid species originally hostile to humans and ruled by verious queens.

planets.name: Fifth Planet
planets.description: A larger rocky planet with no hydrosphere and a thin atmosphere. Temepratures during day barely above freezing.

other_objects.name: Crateris station
other_objects.description: An orbital station with around 50 thousand population.

planets.name: Sixth Planet
planets.description: A small gas giant with at least two dozen moons.

moons.name: Hades / NU-53253235
moons.description: A decaying moon and an ancient construct.

other_objects.name: Debris cloud
other_objects.description: A massive debris cloud created by a colission between two o the gas giant's moons at some point in the past.

planets.name: Seventh planet
planets.description: A very small ice planet in the outer region of the system.

planets.name: Eighth planet
planets.description: A very small ice planet in the outer region of the system.

planets.name: Ninth planet
planets.description: A very small ice planet in the outer region of the system.

planets.name: Mercury
planets.description: The innermost and smallest of the eight planets, orbiting the Sun every 88 days at an average distance of 0.39 AU. Airless and heavily cratered, it has the widest surface temperature swing in the Solar system, from about -180C at night to 430C at midday. It has no natural moons and the most eccentric, tilted orbit of the classical planets.

planets.name: Venus
planets.description: The second planet from the Sun, close to Earth in size and mass but wrapped in a thick CO2 atmosphere that traps heat in a runaway greenhouse effect, making it the hottest planet in the Solar system at roughly 465C even though it orbits farther out than Mercury. It rotates slowly and retrograde, so a single Venusian day (243 Earth days) is longer than its year (225 Earth days). No natural moons.

planets.name: Earth
planets.description: The third planet from the Sun. Ancestral human homeworld. Currently the capital of the Solar Imperium.

moons.name: Luna
moons.description: The seat of power of Empress Psi of the Solar Imperium.

planets.name: Mars
planets.description: The fourth planet, nicknamed the 'Red Planet' for the iron oxide dust covering its surface. Home to Olympus Mons, the largest volcano in the Solar system, and Valles Marineris, a canyon system over 4,000 km long. Its thin CO2 atmosphere and polar ice caps preserve evidence of a wetter, warmer ancient past.

moons.name: Phobos
moons.description: The larger and inner of Mars's two moons, orbiting so close (about 9,400 km) that it completes an orbit in under 8 hours, faster than Mars itself rotates. Its orbit is decaying; it is expected to break apart or crash into Mars within 30-50 million years.

moons.name: Deimos
moons.description: The smaller and outer of Mars's two moons, roughly 12 km across with a smooth, dust-covered surface. Takes about 30 hours to complete an orbit. Like Phobos, likely a captured asteroid.

planets.name: Ceres
planets.description: The largest object in the main asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter, and the only dwarf planet of the inner Solar system. About 940 km across, it holds a significant reservoir of water ice - possibly more fresh water than Earth - and shows evidence of a subsurface ocean and past cryovolcanic activity.

planets.name: Jupiter
planets.description: The fifth planet and by far the largest in the Solar system - a gas giant more massive than all the other planets combined, with a diameter about 11 times Earth's. Famous for the Great Red Spot, a storm that has raged for centuries, and the strongest planetary magnetic field in the Solar system. At least 95 known moons, including the four large Galilean moons discovered by Galileo in 1610. Recently under Princess Celestia as Duchess of Jupiter before she escaped.

moons.name: Io
moons.description: The innermost Galilean moon and the most volcanically active body in the Solar system, with hundreds of active volcanoes driven by tidal heating from Jupiter's gravity. Its sulfur-coated surface gives it a mottled yellow-orange color.

moons.name: Europa
moons.description: An icy Galilean moon with a smooth, fractured surface hiding a liquid saltwater ocean beneath its ice shell, thought to hold more water than all of Earth's oceans combined. One of the most promising places in the Solar system to search for life.

moons.name: Ganymede
moons.description: The largest moon in the Solar system, bigger than the planet Mercury, and the only moon known to generate its own magnetic field. Believed to hide a subsurface saltwater ocean beneath its icy crust.

moons.name: Callisto
moons.description: The outermost Galilean moon and one of the most heavily cratered bodies in the Solar system, with an ancient, geologically inactive surface dating back roughly 4 billion years.

planets.name: Saturn
planets.description: The sixth planet, a gas giant famous for its spectacular ring system of ice particles and rocky debris. The least dense planet in the Solar system - it would float in water - with roughly 146 known moons, including Titan, the second-largest moon in the Solar system.

moons.name: Mimas
moons.description: The smallest and innermost of Saturn's major moons, nicknamed the 'Death Star moon' for the enormous Herschel crater that dominates one hemisphere.

moons.name: Enceladus
moons.description: A small, icy moon with a subsurface ocean and geysers of water vapor and ice erupting from its south pole, feeding Saturn's E ring. One of the leading candidates for finding extraterrestrial microbial life.

moons.name: Tethys
moons.description: An icy moon dominated by the giant Odysseus crater and Ithaca Chasma, a vast canyon system that wraps most of the way around the moon.

moons.name: Dione
moons.description: An icy moon with a heavily cratered surface and bright, wispy fractures ('tiger stripes') on its trailing hemisphere caused by past tectonic activity.

moons.name: Rhea
moons.description: Saturn's second-largest moon, an icy, heavily cratered body that may have a faint ring system of its own - the first potential rings found around a moon.

moons.name: Titan
moons.description: Saturn's largest moon and the second-largest in the Solar system, and the only moon with a dense atmosphere (thicker than Earth's). Its surface has lakes and seas of liquid methane and ethane, making it the only other body known to have stable liquid on its surface.

moons.name: Hyperion
moons.description: Hyperion is the eighth-largest moon of Saturn. It is distinguished by its highly irregular shape, chaotic rotation, low density, and unusual sponge-like appearance. It was the first non-rounded moon to be discovered.

moons.name: Iapetus
moons.description: Saturn's third-largest moon, notable for its extreme two-toned coloring - one hemisphere dark as coal, the other bright ice - and a mysterious equatorial ridge that gives it a walnut-like shape.

planets.name: Uranus
planets.description: The seventh planet, an ice giant tipped on its side with an axial tilt of about 98 degrees, so it essentially rolls around the Sun rather than spinning upright. Its pale blue-green color comes from methane in its atmosphere; it has a faint ring system and 28 known moons, most named after characters from Shakespeare and Pope.

moons.name: Miranda
moons.description: The smallest of Uranus's major moons but the most geologically bizarre, with a jumbled surface of canyons, ridges, and terraces up to 20 km deep - among the most varied terrain of any body in the Solar system.

moons.name: Ariel
moons.description: One of Uranus's major moons, with a relatively young, bright surface marked by canyons and valleys suggesting past geological activity.

moons.name: Umbriel
moons.description: The darkest of Uranus's major moons, heavily cratered with an ancient, largely unchanged surface.

moons.name: Titania
moons.description: The largest moon of Uranus, with a surface of canyons and fault scarps indicating past internal geological activity.

moons.name: Oberon
moons.description: The outermost of Uranus's major moons, heavily cratered with a mountain roughly 11 km high, one of the tallest known in the Solar system.

planets.name: Neptune
planets.description: The eighth and outermost planet, an ice giant with the strongest sustained winds in the Solar system, reaching over 2,000 km/h. Its deep blue color comes from atmospheric methane. Neptune was the first planet located through mathematical prediction rather than direct observation, in 1846.

moons.name: Triton
moons.description: Neptune's largest moon and the only large moon in the Solar system to orbit its planet retrograde (opposite to the planet's rotation), strongly suggesting it is a captured Kuiper Belt object. Geologically active, with nitrogen geysers erupting through its icy surface.

planets.name: Pluto
planets.description: A dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt, once considered the ninth planet until its 2006 reclassification. Roughly two-thirds the diameter of Earth's Moon, it has a heart-shaped plain of nitrogen ice called Tombaugh Regio and a thin, seasonal atmosphere. Pluto and its largest moon, Charon, are close enough in size that they orbit a shared point of gravity outside Pluto itself, sometimes described as a double dwarf-planet system.

moons.name: Charon
moons.description: Pluto's largest moon, about half of Pluto's diameter, making it the largest satellite relative to its parent body in the Solar system. Pluto and Charon are mutually tidally locked, each always showing the same face to the other.

planets.name: Haumea
planets.description: An elongated, egg-shaped dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt, spinning so fast (one rotation in about 4 hours) that it has been stretched into its unusual shape. It has a thin ring system and two known moons, Hi'iaka and Namaka.

planets.name: Makemake
planets.description: A reddish-brown dwarf planet in the Kuiper Belt, the second-brightest Kuiper Belt object as seen from Earth after Pluto. Has one known moon, informally nicknamed MK 2.

planets.name: Eris
planets.description: The most massive known dwarf planet, orbiting in the scattered disk beyond the Kuiper Belt. Its discovery in 2005, and the realization that it rivaled Pluto in size, directly led to the 2006 redefinition of 'planet' that reclassified both as dwarf planets. Has one known moon, Dysnomia.

other_objects.name: Main Asteroid Belt
other_objects.description: A torus-shaped region between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter, roughly 2.2 to 3.2 AU from the Sun, containing millions of rocky asteroids left over from planetesimals that never coalesced into a planet due to Jupiter's gravitational influence. Ceres, the region's only dwarf planet, holds about a third of the belt's total mass.

other_objects.name: Kuiper Belt
other_objects.description: A vast, disc-shaped region of icy bodies beyond Neptune's orbit, extending from about 30 to 50 AU from the Sun. Similar to the asteroid belt but far larger and made mainly of ices such as methane, ammonia, and water. Home to the dwarf planets Pluto, Haumea, and Makemake, and the source of many short-period comets.

other_objects.name: Debris field
other_objects.description: A debris field from the battle between the human resistance and the Entity, during which both were destroyed. Currently infested with rogue autonomous units controlled by a human fleet, GAI Athena gone mad.

other_objects.name: A3123Y
other_objects.description: An asteroid base built by the first A3123Y Alpha NAI subcore. Equipped with a wormhole drive.

planets.name: Meltisar Gas Giant
planets.description: Close-orbiting gas giant; the inhabited moon Meltisar orbits it.

moons.name: Meltisar
moons.description: Inhabited moon, capital world of Meltisar. [Note: the order of the moons except the 3rd one not confirmed.]

Gliese 1143.description:
Gliese 1143 A
Type: K4 V (Orange Dwarf). Mass: 0.539   M ☉ . Luminosity: 0.095   L ☉ . Temperature: 4,250 K

Gliese 1143 B
Type: M-dwarf (Red Dwarf). Separation: 67 AU. Orbital period: Several hundred years.

planets.name: Gliese 1143Ab
planets.description: A gas giant. Orbital Distance: 0.50 AU. Orbital Period: 180 Earth Days. Approximately 1.2 Jupiter masses. Radius: 71,500 km. Strong magnetosphere that effectively deflects high-pressure solar winds. Abundant Helium-3 composition in the upper atmosphere. Additionally, Deuterium/Tritium present in processable quantities. Krypton and Xenon which are critical for transplutonic synthesis also present.

moons.name: 1143Ab I
moons.description:
A volcanic moon heated by extreme tidal stress with a surface tectonically ravaged with constant earthquakes and volcanic eruptions
Resources: Uraninite, Thorite, Monazite, Beryllium, Lithium, Iron, Nickel, Cobalt, Silicates

moons.name: 1143Ab II
moons.description:
A rocky moon with an inactive tectonics present an opportunity for deep core mining for a multitude of common elements.
Resources: Copper, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Palladium, Iridium, Tantalum, Niobium

moons.name: 1143Ab III
moons.description:
A rocky moon with a collapsed frozen carbon dioxide atmosphere, constantly melting and refreezing with its day-night cycle. Cryovolcanic activity presents opportunities for extracting rare superconducting materials in high quantities.
Resources: Water Ice, Methane, Phosphates

moons.name: 1143Ab IV
moons.description:
A nitrogen-carbon dioxide moon with a strong magnetic field and trace oxygen.Not tidally locked unlike the other moons. A magnetic field that deflects radiation. A gravity of 0.5 g and an average temperature around the equator ranging between -5 and 25 degrees Celsius.
Resources: Carbon Dioxide, Nitrogen, Neodymium, Yttrium, Cerium, Magnesium, Silicates, Base Metals, Uranium, Thorium, Zeolites, Graphene Precursor Materials

other_objects.name: Ring
other_objects.description: A material ring around 1143Ab gas giant.
